LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF/CROSS DEFENDANT ANDREA LEUNG, TRUSTEE OF THE JEFFREY CHIN TRUST'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT OF NELLY CHIN. NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - THE DEMURRER TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT IS OVERRULED. THE STATUTE OF FRAUDS (CC 1624(A)(1)) BARS AN ORAL CONTRACT THAT "BY ITS TERMS IS NOT TO BE PERFORMED WITHIN A YEAR OF THE MAKING THEREOF." THE ALLEGATIONS OF THE CROSS COMPLAINT DO NOT CONCLUSIVELY SHOW THAT THE CONTRACT RUNS AFOUL OF THE STATUTE OF FRAUDS. THERE IS A FACTUAL ISSUE REGARDING THE PRECISE TERMS OF THE AGREEMENT. EVEN IF THE CONTRACT WAS FOR PERSONAL SERVICES AND TERMINATED UPON DEATH, PARAGRAPH 13 ALLEGES THAT THE PARTIES "REITERATED" THE CONTRACT AND PARAGRAPH 27 ALLEGES THAT THEY "VERBALLY AFFIRMED" THE CONTRACT. ACCORDINGLY, THE PARTIES RATIFIED THE PRIOR CONTRACT AND/OR ENTERED INTO A NEW AGREEMENT. THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS ARGUMENT FAILS BECAUSE PARAGRAPH 17 ALLEGES THAT THE BREACH OCCURRED WITHIN THE LAST TWO YEARS PRIOR TO THE FILING OF THE ACTION. THE DEMURRER TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. CONVERSION AND FRAUD ARE DISTINCT CAUSES OF ACTION AND CONVERSION DOES NOT NEED TO BE PLEADED WITH THE SAME PARTICULARITY AS AN INTENTIONAL MISREPRESENTATION CLAIM. THERE IS NO STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS ISSUE BECAUSE PARAGRAPH 33 ALLEGES THAT THE CONVERSION WAS DISCOVERED WITHIN THE PAST 3 YEARS PRIOR TO THE FILING OF THE ACTION. THE COURT OVERRULES THE DEMURRER TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION. NOTHING IN THE CROSS-COMPLAINT SHOWS THAT THE CLAIM IS TIME BARRED. IT IS UNCLEAR WHEN MS. CHIN MADE THE EXPENDITURES AND PARAGRAPH 17 INDICATES THAT THERE IS NO STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS ISSUE. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-01-2013 Set For Trial On Feb-18-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-11-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF/CROSS DEFENDANT ANDREA LEUNG, TRUSTEE OF THE JEFFREY CHIN TRUST'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT OF NELLY CHIN; the demurrer to the first cause of action for breach of contract is overruled. The statute of frauds (CC 1624(a)(1)) bars an oral contract that "by its terms is not to be performed within a year of the making thereof." The allegations of the cross complaint do not conclusively show that the contract runs afoul of the statute of frauds. There is a factual issue regarding the precise terms of the agreement. Even if the contract was for personal services and terminated upon death, paragraph 13 alleges that the parties "reiterated" the contract and paragraph 27 alleges that they "verbally affirmed" the contract. Accordingly, the parties ratified the prior contract and/or entered into a new agreement. The statute of limitations argument fails because paragraph 17 alleges that the breach occurred within the last two years prior to the filing of the action. The demurrer to the second cause of action is overruled. Conversion and fraud are distinct causes of action and conversion does not need to be pleaded with the same particularity as an intentional misrepresentation claim. There is no statute of limitations issue because paragraph 33 alleges that the conversion was discovered within the past 3 years prior to the filing of the action. The Court overrules the demurrer to the third cause of action. Nothing in the cross-complaint shows that the claim is time barred. It is unclear when Ms. Chin made the expenditures and paragraph 17 indicates that there is no statute of limitations issue. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185.
